topic: A person finds out their partner has been learning their native language
raw_output:
[story 1]

The silence in the apartment had become a heavy blanket. Every shared meal, every casual conversation, felt like navigating a foreign landscape. I watched Elias, his brow furrowed in concentration as he traced characters on his tablet, a language I’d only vaguely recognized from a travel documentary years ago. A knot tightened in my chest, a dull, persistent ache that refused to ease. I tried to initiate a discussion about our weekend plans, but the words felt clumsy, inadequate. I kept glancing at him, searching for a flicker of shared understanding, but his focus remained entirely on the script glowing on the screen. I retreated to the sofa, picking at a loose thread on the cushion, the urge to simply disappear overwhelming.

[story 2]

It started subtly—a phrase slipped out in a moment of fatigue, a melodic cadence that didn't belong to our usual rhythm. Then, the videos started appearing on his phone, intricate lessons, vocabulary lists. I found myself shrinking away from him, acutely aware of the gulf opening between us. When he spoke that new tongue, his eyes held a faraway light, a world I wasn't invited into. I would nod, offering the polite, empty acknowledgment of someone watching a performance, while inside, a frantic energy churned. I started spending hours in my room, rereading old books, anything familiar, trying to anchor myself against the tide of this unfamiliar development.

[story 3]

The evening unfolded like a slow-motion train wreck. We were supposed to be watching a movie, but Elias was lost, murmuring softly to himself, his fingers dancing over the keyboard, forming complex sentences. I felt a profound sense of isolation, as if I were standing on the shore while he was sailing toward an uncharted island. My hands clenched in my lap, the skin growing tight with tension. I tried to interject, to ask what he was studying, but the words caught in my throat, lodging there like shards of glass. I just watched the way his lips moved, the earnestness in his expression, and felt a deep, hollow resonance of being utterly separate from the current reality of our shared life.
